q: As it was fine, I went for a walk.
o: 1. It being fine, I went for a walk.
2. It wasing fine, I went for a walk.

q: When Jane cleans the room, her sister washes the dishes.
o: 1. Jane cleaning the room, her sister washes the dishes.
2. Cleaning the room, her sister washes the dishes.

q: As it snowed a lot, we have to walk to school.
o: 1. It snowing a lot, we have to walk to school.
2. It having snowed a lot, we have to walk to school.

q: As it is Christmas, all the people are joyful.
o: 1. It being Christmas, all the people are joyful.
2. It is been Christmas, all the people are joyful.

q: Though I studied hard last night, I can’t answer the question.
o: 1. Though having studied hard last night, I can’t answer the question.
2. Though studying hard last night, I can’t answer the question.

q: She studies English with her MP3 ***.
He fell asleep on the sofa with the TV ***.
He takes a walk with his dog *** him.
He was studying English with his friend *** a book.
She cooked dinner, with her baby ***.
Don't speak with your mouth ***.
generally speaking
frankly speaking
judging from
considering that
strictly speaking
roughly speaking
talking of
compared with
As there are lots of homework, he can't watch TV.
o: 1. There being lots of homework, he can't watch TV.
2. Being lots of homework, he can't watch TV.
